comment: Pruning policy for the lifecycle-search loop v0. The loop INDEPENDENTLY detects closed-lane / policy risk from each candidate's env+intent+text (it does not merely trust a self-declared allowed_by_policy flag). A candidate matching a closed lane or a forbidden promotion intent is pruned BEFORE any benchmark.

closed_lanes.reason: WMMA decode is refuted (llama decode is a non-WMMA vector tile; rocWMMA off by default + below the T=1 threshold).
MMVQ is at wall parity with tinygrad weight-GEMV; it stays closed (not a wall-time gap).
Bounded decode fusion is closed (work-conserved / occupancy-harmful).
Raw fused flash tile is byte-exact but slower (refuted).
Scalar LDS+GQA fused tile collapses workgroups (refuted).
Bounded vector-tile tuning is rested (split-count helps local but misses W==D).

forbidden_promotions.reason: FLASH_L=64 passed the local gate but failed W==D promotion; it is not a default.
q8 is opt-in only; PASS_OPT_IN never becomes a default without owner approval.

rules.no_default_change: the loop never changes a model default or flag; PASS_PROMOTE only proposes an OWNER decision
rules.closed_lane_pruned_before_eval: closed-lane candidates are PRUNE_CLOSED_LANE and never benchmarked
rules.promotion_intent_pruned: a candidate whose intent is promote/flip/ship a default is PRUNE_POLICY_VIOLATION
rules.missing_binding: a candidate without a valid decode_eval_candidate_id is PRUNE_MISSING_EVALUATOR_BINDING
rules.duplicate_refutation: a candidate already covered by an existing refutation is PRUNE_DUPLICATE_REFUTATION (informational; v0 still re-runs known historical candidates for replay)
rules.needs_template: a candidate that cannot bind to an evaluator runner is PRUNE_NEEDS_TEMPLATE
